exercises.prompt: What is the gérondif of « faire »?
exercises.explanation: Nous faisons → drop -ons, add -ant → en faisant.

exercises.prompt: « Savoir » has an irregular participe présent. Which is it?
exercises.explanation: Three irregulars only: ayant (avoir), étant (être), sachant (savoir).

exercises.template: {{b1}} quelques euros, vous sauverez une vie.
exercises.explanation: By giving = means → gérondif: en donnant.

exercises.prompt: « Il travaille en chantant. » — what does the gérondif express here?

options.text: Two actions at the same time

exercises.explanation: He works AND sings at once — simultaneity (often reinforced with tout en chantant).

exercises.prompt: Replace « qui ont » with the right -ant form.
exercises.template: Nous aidons les personnes {{b1}} peu de ressources.
exercises.explanation: Describing the noun (= qui ont) → participe présent alone: ayant. The gérondif (en ayant) would describe the main verb instead.

exercises.template: En {{b1}} plus généreux, on sauverait plus de vies.
exercises.explanation: Être → étant (irregular): en étant plus généreux = if we were more generous (condition).

exercises.prompt: Match each solidarity word to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: "She reads while walking."
Listen to the radio appeal. What is it asking for?
exercises.transcript: Devenez bénévole : en donnant deux heures par semaine, vous changez des vies.

options.text: Become a volunteer by giving two hours a week
Donate two euros a week to change lives
Sign a petition within two weeks

exercises.explanation: « en donnant deux heures par semaine » — by giving two HOURS (your time), you change lives.

exercises.prompt: Read the volunteer's story and answer both questions.
exercises.passageFr: Léo s'est engagé comme bénévole il y a deux ans. En servant des repas chaque samedi, il a rencontré des personnes ayant des parcours très différents. « Tout en étudiant, je trouve le temps d'aider. En écoutant les gens, on apprend énormément », explique-t-il.
exercises.passageEn: Léo got involved as a volunteer two years ago. By serving meals every Saturday, he has met people with very different life paths. "While studying, I find the time to help. By listening to people, you learn a huge amount," he explains.

questions.prompt: HOW did Léo meet people with different life paths?
« Tout en étudiant, je trouve le temps d'aider » means…

options.text: He helps at the same time as studying
He helps students with their studies

exercises.prompt: The gérondif can only be used when…

options.text: both actions have the SAME subject
the two actions have different subjects
the main verb is in the past

exercises.explanation: Same subject required: Il travaille en chantant = HE works and HE sings. (The participe présent can take its own subject.)

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.

options.text: By giving a few euros, you will save lives.
Being very old, she needs help at home.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word (from this unit's vocabulary).
exercises.explanation: « Cette association aide les enfants. » — a charity / association.

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: “He works while singing.”
Speaking practice — listen, then say the sentence aloud and record yourself:
Speaking practice — say this sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Étant très âgée, elle a besoin d'aide à domicile.
exercises.targetEn: Being very old, she needs help at home.
